sections.paragraph: f. coitus, from the root אָנָה No. 2. Piel, to cause to meet, which is applied to copulation. It is once used of the lust of the wild she-ass, Jerem. 2:24 . Not less suitably, N. G. Schrœder (Observatt. ad Origg. Heb. page 10) derives the signification of lust from the root انى to be hot (compare פָּחַז).
